summary: Built-in example development workflow profiles for agent-os.
override_policy: A direct user instruction for the current repo or session overrides the active profile unless it would discard work, publish externally, or perform a destructive operation.

axes.dock: Durable agent role, authority boundary, handoff contract, stop conditions, and lifecycle hooks.
axes.entry_path: Active capability layer for the current task, such as Agent harness, AOS developer, testing, visual diagnostics, or user-input diagnostics.
axes.workflow_profile: Development branch, commit, review, PR, and release posture.

profiles.name: Hybrid Trunk
intended_for: agent relay review without mandatory pull requests
profiles.review_default: local tests, agent relay review, or human review when risk warrants it
profiles.commit_strategy: small frequent scoped commits directly on main
profiles.branch_strategy: use short-lived branches sparingly as a safety valve
branch_required_for: external collaboration requiring a pull request
agent_guidance: Prefer staying on main when the worktree is clean and the task is small.
Keep main releasable by running focused verification before checkpointing.
Use branches only when they reduce real risk or preserve unrelated work.

profiles.name: GitHub Flow
intended_for: projects using lightweight pull request review
profiles.review_default: lightweight pull request review before merge
profiles.commit_strategy: scoped commits on a short-lived branch
profiles.branch_strategy: create one branch per feature, bugfix, refactor, or docs change
branch_optional_for: tiny emergency fix when explicitly approved
agent_guidance: Start from main, create a short-lived branch, and keep it close to main.
Use a pull request for review and merge back into main when ready.

profiles.name: GitFlow
intended_for: projects requiring separated development, release, and production branches
profiles.review_default: review before merging into develop, release, hotfix, or main branches
profiles.commit_strategy: scoped commits on feature, release, or hotfix branches
profiles.branch_strategy: use named develop, feature/*, release/*, and hotfix/* branches
agent_guidance: Do not work directly on main for normal development.
Branch day-to-day work from develop unless the task is a production hotfix.
Use release branches to stabilize releases before merging to main.
